title: Pennywise "We All Float" Bathtub Drawing
about: The Pennywise "We All Float" Bathtub Drawing refers to a piece of art based on the Stephen King horror story IT , showing Pennywise the clown leaning over a bathtub naked, his posterior floating on the water, with the phrase "We all float!" written in steam on glass in the background. The art was originally created and posted by Instagram user thewerkroom.shop in October 2022. In December 2025 and January 2026, the art became the subject of memes as a reaction image and exploitable format. It also became the subject of redraws , in which Pennywise is replaced with other characters.
origin:
The Pennywise in the bathtub art was posted by Instagram [1] user thewerkroom.shop on October 11th, 2022, garnering about three likes in four days. The art, which shows Pennywise taking a bath with his posterior in the air, was created for the "Halloqueer Collection," a collection of products featuring the drawing.

It is unconfirmed when the art was originally used as a meme. The earliest known example is a parody art tutorial video by TikToker [2] @chase47k posted on December 7th, 2025, in which they show a step-by-step tutorial on how to draw a clown, ending with the Pennywise art breaking through the original art, garnering over 60,000 views in about two months.

On December 18th, TikToker [3] @art.pabl2 posted a similar video that garnered significantly more interactions, garnering over 2.5 million views in a month.
